mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for corporations and increases the present value of future earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate indicates improving labor market health, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to show statistically significant positive 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.10) following periods where UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales, combined with an accommodative federal funds rate, indicates strong consumer demand supported by low borrowing costs, creating a favorable environment for corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.01%) when both RSAFS shows positive monthly growth and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an early-cycle expansion signals increasing manufacturing activity and improving corporate profitability, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns as economic growth strengthens.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) in periods where INDPRO rises by more than 1% month-over-month for two consecutive months, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent housing starts reflect ongoing construction activity and future demand for related goods and services, providing a foundational positive contribution to economic activity and supporting S&P 500 performance over short horizons.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns show no positive correlation (Pearson r < 0.05) with HOUST levels over any 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in consumer price inflation signals potential for central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month for three consecutive months, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth coupled with robust retail sales indicates a healthy and expanding consumer base with increasing purchasing power, directly supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k MoM AND RSAFS rises > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) when PAYEMS rises by over 200k MoM and RSAFS rises by over 0.5% MoM, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed contraction in real GDP signals a recessionary environment, leading to widespread corporate earnings downgrades and increased risk aversion, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 0% QoQ
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 30% of periods following a confirmed quarter-over-quarter decline in GDPC1, this hypothesis is rejected.
